note: Projectile sprites are small (canonical 8×8 or 16×16), short animations (1-4 frames), and don't need identity preservation across variants. ERNIE canvas 1024² is WAY oversized — output is cropped to the target cell size. Lowest-risk ERNIE workflow in the library.
_data_driven_design: Based on corpus analysis (49 projectile animations, 14 essences): canonical sizes are 16×16 (37%), 8×8 (33%), 16×8 (12%), 48×8 (8%). Dominant sub_kinds: special_attack_proj (17), melee_thrown (4), gun_proj (4), missile (2), explosive (1). 21 entries are untagged — JOB-INT-15 backfill candidate.

gun_proj.description: Firearm pellet / beam — generic small shot. Player or enemy.
gun_proj.notes: Typically single-frame with motion blur optional. Directional variants achievable via runtime rotation rather than sprite-per-direction.

special_attack_proj.description: Character-unique projectile (hadoken / charge-shot / wave-beam). Identity-tied to a specific character, larger + more detailed.
special_attack_proj.notes: Frame-count 4 covers the typical travel animation (pulsing or energy-trail). Character-specific → seed per character, not per projectile.

missile.description: Homing / long-range projectile with distinct head + trail.
missile.notes: Often has a visible trail that needs to read during scrolling. Frame-count 4 for the thrust-pulse animation.

melee_thrown.description: Thrown melee weapon (dagger / axe / cross / shuriken). Ballistic arc or straight-line.
melee_thrown.notes: Usually has a rotation animation — 4 frames covers a 90° or full rotation. Pre-rotated variants beat runtime-rotate for 8-bit feel.

explosive.description: Area-damage projectile (grenade / bomb / explosive arrow). Terminates in explosion_vfx spawn.
explosive.notes: Frame-count 4 covers the fuse-pulse travel animation (bright-dim alternation). Impact spawns an explosion_vfx from the vfx_library.

palette_presets.yellow_pellet: bright yellow with white center core — classic 8-bit blaster shot
palette_presets.red_energy: red-orange energy ball — often enemy shot
palette_presets.white_missile: white metallic missile with orange exhaust trail
palette_presets.brown_dagger: brown-silver thrown dagger with blade highlight
palette_presets.grey_bomb: dark grey metal bomb with red fuse light
palette_presets.purple_magic: purple magical spell projectile with sparkle

implementation_notes: Identity preservation is NOT required per-projectile (unlike character workflow). Seed is just for reproducibility.
The ERNIE 1024² → crop to 8×8 means most of the canvas is discarded — that's fine. Prompt the projectile centered on a magenta-chromakey background + use mode='icon' for transparent cutout.
Single-frame projectiles (gun_proj) skip the strip-assembly step.
Multi-frame projectiles (4-frame travel anims) get assembled into a 4x1_horizontal_strip via sprite_sheet_asm.assemble_strip().
Runtime handles direction: sprites are always produced facing E (right); flip_horizontal handles W, rotation handles N/S/diagonals. Avoids a 4× or 8× sprite-gen multiplier.
